title: Module 8: Confidence Scaling
learning_objective: Use confidence rulers to assess readiness for action; Explore what supports current confidence; Identify small steps that build confidence; Avoid over-reassurance or minimizing doubts
scenario_context: Learn to assess and explore confidence for weight loss changes using scaling questions

persona_config.name: That's
persona_config.background: Learn to assess and explore confidence for weight loss changes using scaling questions

conf_1.persona_text: I want to walk for 30 minutes, 3 times a week. That's my goal.

choice_points.option_text: That's a great goal! You can definitely do this if you put your mind to it.

feedback.immediate: This cheerleading approach doesn't actually assess or build genuine confidence.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: On a scale from 0 to 10, how confident do you feel about achieving that goal?

feedback.immediate: Perfect use of scaling to assess actual confidence level.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: Walking 30 minutes is quite a lot if you're currently sedentary. Are you sure you can do that?

feedback.immediate: This introduces doubt instead of exploring the customer's actual confidence.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

conf_2a.persona_text: I hope so. I've tried to start exercise programs before and they haven't worked out.

choice_points.option_text: You just need to believe in yourself more. You've got this!

feedback.immediate: This dismisses legitimate concerns and doesn't build real confidence.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: Past attempts haven't gone as you'd hoped. On a scale of 0 to 10, how confident do you feel about this attempt?

feedback.immediate: Good - acknowledges the history and now assesses actual confidence.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

conf_2b.persona_text: Honestly? Maybe a 5. I want to do it, but I'm not sure I'll stick with it.

choice_points.option_text: A 5 is pretty low. You need to get that number up before you can start.

feedback.immediate: This judges the score negatively and creates pressure rather than exploration.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: A 5 - so you have some confidence, but also some doubts. What makes it a 5 and not a 0?

feedback.immediate: Excellent - accepts the score and explores what confidence already exists.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: Don't worry, your confidence will go up once you start seeing results.

feedback.immediate: This skips over the important work of understanding and building confidence now.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

conf_2c.persona_text: Well, I'm not sure now. Maybe I should start with something smaller?

choice_points.option_text: Let's think about what you actually feel confident about. On a scale of 0 to 10, how confident are you about taking some steps toward more activity?

feedback.immediate: Good recovery - moves away from doubt-inducing approach to assessment.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: Yes, 30 minutes is probably too much. Let's aim for 10 minutes instead.

feedback.immediate: This takes over decision-making rather than exploring the customer's confidence.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

conf_3a.persona_text: Well, I've got all the tools - a pair of legs, comfortable shoes. And I've done it before when I was younger. I know I can walk.

choice_points.option_text: So you have the physical ability and past success to draw on. What accounts for the other side - what makes it not an 8 or 9?

feedback.immediate: Perfect - affirms strengths and explores what's holding confidence back.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: Right, so there's no reason you can't do it. You just need to start.

feedback.immediate: This dismisses the legitimate concerns that keep confidence at 5.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: Those are great resources. What's worked for you before when you've stayed active?

feedback.immediate: Good - builds on strengths and explores what has worked before.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

conf_4a.persona_text: I guess it's the sticking with it part. I tend to start strong and then life gets in the way - work stress, family stuff. Then I stop.

choice_points.option_text: What would help move your confidence up even one point - say from a 5 to a 6?

feedback.immediate: Excellent - focuses on realistic, small improvements in confidence.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: You need to make exercise a priority. Put it in your calendar and treat it like any other appointment.

feedback.immediate: This jumps to solutions without exploring what would actually help the customer.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: Life gets in the way for everyone. You just have to push through it.

feedback.immediate: This minimizes real challenges instead of problem-solving collaboratively.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

conf_4b.persona_text: When I was younger, I had friends who I'd work out with. We'd meet up and it was social, not just exercise. That made it fun.

choice_points.option_text: Having that social connection made a real difference. What might help recreate something like that now?

feedback.immediate: Good - honors past success and explores how to apply it now.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: Great! So you should find a walking buddy or join a group. Problem solved.

feedback.immediate: This rushes past the customer's own problem-solving process.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

conf_5a.persona_text: Maybe if I started with just 10 minutes instead of 30? That feels more doable. I could commit to that.

choice_points.option_text: Starting with 10 minutes is a smart way to build confidence. On that shorter walk goal, where's your confidence now?

feedback.immediate: Perfect - affirms the adaptive planning and reassesses confidence on the revised goal.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: But 10 minutes won't really give you the financial benefits you need. You should aim for at least 20.

feedback.immediate: This dismisses the customer's confidence-appropriate goal.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

conf_5b.persona_text: Actually, my neighbor mentioned she walks in the mornings. Maybe I could ask if she'd want company. That might help.

choice_points.option_text: That could provide the support and accountability you're looking for. How confident would you feel about reaching out to her?

feedback.immediate: Excellent - links to what matters and checks confidence on the specific action.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: Perfect! You should definitely do that. Having a walking partner is the key to success.

feedback.immediate: This pressures rather than supporting the customer's self-directed planning.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

conf_end_good.persona_text: With 10 minutes and maybe a walking buddy? I'd say my confidence is more like a 7 or 8 now. That feels realistic.

conf_end_poor.persona_text: I guess I'll try, but I'm not feeling great about it. Maybe I'm just not ready for this.
